findings.summary: Inline Python in bash single-quoted string used backslash-escaped quotes — SyntaxError. Bash single quotes pass backslashes literally to Python.
findings.evidence: gather-state.sh:18 — f-string with escaped quotes hit SyntaxError on first test run
findings.proposed_fix: Rule: for inline Python in bash, use .format() not f-strings with nested quotes. Or write a separate .py helper file. Extends existing >10-line rule to any Python with dict access.

findings.summary: Two parallel research agents (Explore + claude-code-guide) returned overlapping SKILL.md format documentation. One agent would have sufficed.
findings.evidence: Both agents returned ~4k tokens of overlapping SKILL.md frontmatter and best practices content
findings.proposed_fix: For best-practices questions, dispatch one claude-code-guide + one Explore with distinct scopes, not two agents on same topic.
